text:
Once upon a time in a quaint town called Everwood, there lived a peculiar professor named Professor Fuzzywit. He was well-known for his glasses that fogged up instantly upon entering warm weather, his unkempt beard, and his ability to recite poetry about the weather that never matched the actual climate.

One sunny April morning, as Professor Fuzzywit was about to lecture on the art of precipitation, his glasses fogged completely. "Eh, what's a little rain?" he mumbled, looking up from his script as the town quietly whispered amongst itself.

His first lesson was on thunderstorms. He began reciting about the electricity coursing through clouds, the great bolts of lightning, and how storms were nothing to fear, just a bit of nature's fury that turned wheat into gold through the seasons.

As he droned on, a soft rain started to fall, not from the heavens, but from the rainflies Fuzzywit brought in a net. He had always been
